title: One line in my diary every night
summary: Reflect on the habit of writing a single line in a diary each night and how the small notes add up over years.
topic: Writing one line in a diary every night
prompt: Write 8–10 short sentences about a small daily writing habit (a one-line diary, a gratitude note, a list). Describe what you write, when you write it, and what you notice when you read old pages. Use present simple, adverbs of frequency, and 'but'/'because' to link ideas.
hintStarters: Every night, before I turn off the light, I…
I only write one line because…
When I read the old pages, I sometimes…
sampleText: Every night, before I turn off the light, I write one line in a small notebook. I write the date first, and then one true thing about my day. I only write one line because a long diary is hard to keep. Some lines are very ordinary, like 'I drank tea with my sister' or 'The rain was loud all night'. I do not write about big things often. Most days are quiet, but each day still has one small moment. I never miss a night because the notebook sits on my pillow. After a few months, the pages fill up with tiny notes. Sometimes I read the old pages, and I remember days I almost forgot. One short line brings back a whole afternoon. The small notes are not important on their own, but together they become my life. I think a person forgets ninety days but remembers one line from each. My notebook is thin, but it holds more than my memory does.

I write one line in a small notebook

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a long diary is hard to keep

tôi không bao giờ bỏ lỡ một đêm nào

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the pages fill up with tiny notes

gợi lại cả một buổi chiều
gộp lại chúng trở thành cuộc đời tôi

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency for a habit
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng các trạng từ tần suất để mô tả thói quen: 'I only write', 'I never miss', 'Sometimes I read', 'I do not write... often'. Chúng thường đứng trước động từ chính.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use frequency adverbs to describe a habit: 'I only write', 'I never miss', 'Sometimes I read', 'I do not write... often'. They usually go before the main verb.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: 'but' and 'because' to connect ideas
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'because' để nêu lý do ('because a long diary is hard to keep') và 'but' để nối ý trái nhau ('My notebook is thin, but it holds more than my memory does').
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'because' to give a reason ('because a long diary is hard to keep') and 'but' to join contrasting ideas ('My notebook is thin, but it holds more than my memory does').

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi đêm, trước khi tắt đèn, tôi viết một dòng vào một cuốn sổ nhỏ. Tôi viết ngày tháng trước, rồi một điều thật về ngày của mình. Tôi chỉ viết một dòng vì một cuốn nhật ký dài thì khó duy trì. Có những dòng rất bình thường, như 'Tôi uống trà với chị gái' hay 'Mưa to suốt đêm'. Tôi không thường viết về những chuyện lớn. Hầu hết các ngày đều yên tĩnh, nhưng mỗi ngày vẫn có một khoảnh khắc nhỏ. Tôi không bao giờ bỏ lỡ một đêm nào vì cuốn sổ nằm trên gối của tôi. Sau vài tháng, các trang đầy lên với những ghi chú nhỏ xíu. Đôi khi tôi đọc những trang cũ, và tôi nhớ lại những ngày mà tôi suýt quên. Một dòng ngắn gợi lại cả một buổi chiều. Những ghi chú nhỏ tự thân không quan trọng, nhưng gộp lại chúng trở thành cuộc đời tôi. Tôi nghĩ một người quên chín mươi ngày nhưng nhớ một dòng từ mỗi ngày. Cuốn sổ của tôi mỏng, nhưng nó lưu giữ nhiều hơn trí nhớ của tôi.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes your nightly habit most naturally?
options: Every night, before I turn off the light, I writes one line in a small notebook.
Every night, before I turn off the light, I write one line in a small notebook.
Every night, before I turn off the light, one line in a small notebook I write it.
Every night, before I turning off the light, I write one line in a small notebook.
mcQuestions.explanation: With 'I' the verb is 'write' (no -s), and the order is natural.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why you write only one line?
options: I only writes one line because a long diary is hard to keep.
I only write one line because of a long diary is hard to keep.
I only write one line because a long diary is hard to keep.
I only write one line because hard to keep a long diary is it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I write' (no -s), and 'because' is followed by a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: I do not writes about big things often.
I do not write often about big things very.
Often I do not about big things write it.
I do not write about big things often.
mcQuestions.explanation: Negative present simple 'do not write', with 'often' at the end naturally.
mcQuestions.hint: How do you form the negative present simple?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence contrasts quiet days with a small moment?
options: Most days are quiet, but each day still has one small moment.
Most days is quiet, but each day still have one small moment.
Most days are quiet, but each day still has one small moments.
Most days are quiet, but one small moment each day still has it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'days are' (plural) and 'each day has' (singular -s) both agree; 'but' shows contrast.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence explains why you never miss a night?
options: I never miss a night because the notebook sit on my pillow.
I never miss a night because the notebook sits on my pillow.
I never misses a night because the notebook sits on my pillow.
I miss never a night because on my pillow the notebook sits it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I miss' (no -s), 'notebook sits' (singular -s), and 'because' has a full clause.
mcQuestions.hint: Check both verb forms and the clause after 'because'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple: 'After a few months, the pages ___ up with tiny notes.'
options: fills
filling
fill
filled

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes reading the old pages most naturally?
options: Sometimes I reads the old pages, and I remembers days I almost forgot.
Sometimes I read the old pages, and I remember days I almost forget.
Sometimes the old pages I read, and days I almost forgot I remember them.
Sometimes I read the old pages, and I remember days I almost forgot.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'I read' and 'I remember' have no -s; 'forgot' is the correct past for a finished idea.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verbs with 'I' and the past 'forgot'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows how one line recalls a moment?
options: One short line brings back a whole afternoon.
One short line bring back a whole afternoon.
One short line brings back a whole afternoons.
One short line brings back a whole afternoon completely entirely fully.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'brings' agrees with the singular subject 'one short line'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb agreement with the singular subject.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence states how the small notes add up?
options: The small notes is not important on their own, but together they becomes my life.
The small notes are not important on their own, but together they become my life.
The small notes are not important on their own, but together they become my lifes.
The small notes are not important on their own, but my life together they become it.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'notes are' and 'they become' are plural; 'but' shows the contrast.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the plural subjects to their verbs.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best closes the reflection?
options: My notebook is thin, but it hold more than my memory does.
My notebook is thin, but it holds more than my memory do.
My notebook is thin, but it holds more than my memory does.
My notebook thin is, but more than my memory it holds does.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'it holds' and 'my memory does' both take the singular -s correctly.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the verb endings with 'it' and 'my memory'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: If you wrote only one line about today, what would it say, and why is it so easy to forget the ordinary days of our lives?
